misconceptions.name: Boundary Confusion
misconceptions.description: Confusing 'less than' with 'less than or equal to'
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Does the boundary value itself satisfy the condition?

misconceptions.name: Off-by-One Error
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Are you counting both endpoints? Try a small example.
examples: Counting integers from 1 to 10 as 9 instead of 10

misconceptions.name: Percent Base Confusion
misconceptions.description: Using wrong base when calculating percentages
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Percent of WHAT? What is the whole?
examples: Calculating 20% increase on the new value instead of original

misconceptions.name: Order of Operations Error
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Which operation should be done first? Remember BEDMAS/PEMDAS.

misconceptions.name: Fraction Operation Error
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: What is the rule for this operation with fractions?

misconceptions.name: Negative Number Confusion
misconceptions.description: Errors with negative number operations or ordering
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Try placing these on a number line. Which direction is larger?

misconceptions.name: Unit Conversion Error
misconceptions.description: Multiplying when should divide or vice versa
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Are you going to a bigger or smaller unit? Should the number get bigger or smaller?
examples: Dividing when converting cm to m

misconceptions.name: Factor vs Multiple Confusion
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Factors divide INTO a number. Multiples are PRODUCTS of a number. Which is this?
examples: Listing multiples when asked for factors

misconceptions.name: Prime Number Confusion
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: What are ALL the factors? Is there more than 1 and itself?
examples: Missing that 2 is the only even prime

misconceptions.name: Consecutive Integer Error
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: What is special about consecutive numbers? How are they related?
examples: Not recognizing middle = average for consecutive integers
Wrong setup for consecutive even/odd integers

misconceptions.name: Area vs Perimeter Confusion
misconceptions.description: Using wrong formula or confusing the concepts
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Are we measuring the boundary (perimeter) or the space inside (area)?

misconceptions.name: Misread Question
misconceptions.description: Missing key words or conditions in the problem
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Can you read the question again? What EXACTLY is being asked?
examples: Finding any answer instead of specific constraint

misconceptions.name: Decimal Place Error
misconceptions.description: Misaligning decimal points or misunderstanding place value
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: What does each digit represent? Try lining up the decimal points.

misconceptions.name: Average vs Sum Confusion
misconceptions.description: Confusing or incorrectly computing average and sum
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: Average = Sum / Count. Which do you have? Which do you need?
examples: Finding sum when asked for average

misconceptions.name: Complementary Probability Error
misconceptions.description: Not considering all outcomes or using wrong total
misconceptions.coaching_hint: Ask: What are ALL the possible outcomes? Does your answer make sense (between 0 and 1)?
